payload.title: New York State Budget Agreement Slides

evidence_refs.source_quote: New revenues dedicated to MTA in the State Budget
Increase the top rate of the Payroll Mobility Tax (PMT) for the largest businesses in New York City

payload.description: Increase the top rate of the Payroll Mobility Tax (PMT) for the largest businesses in New York City (from 0.34% to 0.60%)
Increase New York City's share of funding for paratransit services to 80%

tool_args.label: State General Fund aid to MTA

payload.description: Provide State General Fund aid to MTA

tool_args.label: Casino license and tax revenue expected 2026

payload.raw_value_text: Expected to start in 2026, $500 - $700 million per year
payload.description: 100% of three downstate casino license fees ($500 mm each) and 40-50% of gaming tax revenue (depending on location)
Reduce the proposed fare increase from 5.5 percent to 4 percent
Add subway service on weekday middays, weekends and weeknights
Safety and fare enforcement investments
Pilot program providing five fare-free bus routes in New York City

tool_args.label: Budget provides balanced solution to MTA fiscal cliff

payload.claim_text: New State and City funding, MTA operating savings, and a reasonable fare and toll change, provide a balanced solution to fully address MTA's fiscal cliff
payload.description: The State Budget Agreement provides a balanced approach to MTA's fiscal cliff through new government funding, operating savings, and a fare/toll change.

tool_args.label: New revenues fully address MTA deficits

payload.claim_text: New revenues dedicated to MTA in the State Budget fully address MTA's remaining deficits
payload.description: The new revenues including PMT increase, paratransit funding increase, state aid, and casino license fees fully address MTA's remaining deficits.

payload.claim_text: Two-year sunset provision (reverts to 50% if not extended)
payload.description: The increased NYC share of paratransit funding to 80% has a two-year sunset provision, reverting to 50% if not extended.

payload.claim_text: Reduce the proposed fare increase from 5.5 percent to 4 percent
payload.description: Budget offsets $65M annually to reduce the proposed fare increase from 5.5% to 4%

tool_args.label: New York State provides funding to MTA

payload.description: New York State Budget provides new revenues dedicated to MTA including PMT increase, paratransit funding, state aid, and casino license fees

tool_args.label: New York City provides paratransit funding

payload.description: Increase New York City's share of funding for paratransit services to 80%

tool_args.label: Budget funds fare-free bus pilot program

payload.description: Budget includes $15 million annually for pilot program providing five fare-free bus routes in New York City
